metrics.hint_bad: Reaching your hand too far forward causes you to lean forward: Relax your arm and let it hang down more naturally, with the ball placed slightly in front of your body.
Too small/too stiff elbow angle: Keep relaxed and open the elbow angle more (≥145°).
Your upper arm is raised too far forward or too high: relax your shoulders and let your hand fall more naturally and vertically.
Knees extending beyond the toes: The center of gravity is too far forward, and the hips should ‘sit back’ (i.e., return to the starting position).
Hip too far forward: Return the center of gravity to the center of the foot, and sit back with your hips for better stability.
Poor forward tilt angle: Maintain a forward tilt of 15°–35° to both protect the ball and allow for immediate start.
Dribbling height is relatively high: mainly below the hip, maintaining control and speed.
Dribbling height is relatively high: mainly below the hip, maintaining control and speed.
